theme: Romantic Love Across Cultures - Choosing Love Over Family Expectations
sources: Love What Matters - Emily Burden (Lebanese/African American, 9/10)
Growing Up Gupta - Amreet and Luis (British Indian/Angolan, 9.5/10)
Al Jazeera - Multiple interfaith couples (Indian Hindu-Muslim, 8.5/10)

emotionalArc.struggle: Couple from different cultures falls in love, faces immediate family resistance and cultural barriers. Forced to hide relationship for years, losing friends and support.
emotionalArc.crisis: Family discovers relationship, threatens to cut ties. Couple must choose between love and family approval. Physical/emotional toll of secrecy (illness, isolation, fear).
emotionalArc.perseverance: Choosing love despite opposition, hiding relationship for years, building life together in secret, facing community disapproval, cultural adaptation struggles after marriage.
emotionalArc.breakthrough: Family gradually accepts after seeing couple's commitment and love. Building bicultural family, finding common ground, cultural differences become richness not division.

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Family members said 'They should still be slaves' - racist comments from grandparents
'I was not allowed to tell my grandparents' - forced secrecy from day one
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Hiding true self, fear of family disapproval, living double life

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'We quickly found ourselves without friends' - social isolation
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Loneliness, standing alone, choosing love over acceptance

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Romantic moment on ice: 'Never forget this moment' - love persisting despite everything
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Finding joy in struggle, love as anchor, beautiful moments in hardship

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Grandfather apologized for family's intolerance after meeting partner - breakthrough
'Do not bring a Black or Muslim person into the family' - explicit family rule
Hid relationship for 5 years with 'constant lying and sneaking around'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Living in fear, exhaustion of secrecy, mental burden

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Diagnosed with Ulcerative Colitis from stress - physical toll of hiding
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Physical consequences of emotional stress, breaking point

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: Dad's initial response: 'You've made your decision; what's the point in meeting him now?'
Luis's three questions: 'What do you want? What does your heart say? What does your intuition tell you?'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Self-discovery, choosing own path, finding voice

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'We have had to leave our families, our homes, our friends – just everything behind'
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Ultimate sacrifice, choosing love over everything, starting over

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'Her brothers had locked her up' - physical imprisonment
'I was a dirty little secret kept from Indians in his community'
'Why can't you stand up for me with your family?' - cultural clash after marriage
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Feeling unsupported, cultural differences, adaptation struggles

keyEmotionalMoments.moment: 'Life broke us in' - gradual acceptance over decades
keyEmotionalMoments.eslResonance: Time healing wounds, patience, eventual understanding

eslResonanceMultipliers: Communication & Language Barriers (language differences, cultural translation)
Belonging & Identity (caught between two cultures, finding identity)
First-Time Courage (choosing love despite family disapproval)
Persistence Despite Setbacks (years of resistance, gradual acceptance)

characterProfile.culturalBackground: Composite: Maya is from traditional immigrant family (Middle Eastern/South Asian background), Alex is from different cultural background (African American/Latinx/European).
characterProfile.ageRange: Late teens to early twenties when relationship starts, spans 5-10 years to show progression.
characterProfile.familyDynamics: Maya's family holds traditional values, explicit rules against interracial/interfaith relationships, strong community pressure.
characterProfile.internalConflict: Torn between love for partner and love for family, fear of rejection, guilt over hiding, exhaustion from secrecy.
characterProfile.externalConflict: Family resistance, community disapproval, losing friends, cultural clashes, forced secrecy, eventual confrontation.
characterProfile.transformation: From hiding and fear to finding voice, choosing love, building life together, family gradual acceptance, becoming bridge between cultures.
